response:
# Triage order — top 10 findings

`crimes verdict --format json` wasn't included in the prompt, so this is ranked by the scan's aggregate risk ordering. Categories adjusted where the finding doesn't fit one of the five named buckets cleanly.

| # | ID | Charge | Category | File | Action |
|---|----|----|----|----|----|
| 1 | crime_00002 | God Function (`generateInvoice`, 204 lines) | structural | `src/billing.ts` | **fix now** |
| 2 | crime_00003 | Temporal Recklessness (8 calls) | structural / testability | `src/date.ts` | **fix now** |
| 3 | crime_00001 | SVG With Embedded Raster | frontend | `src/assets/partner-logo.svg` | **fix now** |
| 4 | crime_00004 | Half-UTC, Half-Local | hot-path/portability (no exact bucket) | `src/date.ts` | **fix now** — bundle with #2 |
| 5 | crime_00005 | Missing Agent Context | IA (agent-readability) | repo root | **fix now** — cheap, unblocks every later edit |
| 6 | crime_00007 | Localhost-on-Disk (`/Users/andrew/…`) | hot-path/portability | `src/api/files.ts` | **fix now** — breaks on any other machine |
| 7 | crime_00006 | Dev-Server URL (`localhost:3000`) | hot-path/portability | `src/api/files.ts` | **fix now** — bundle with #6 |
| 8 | crime_00010 | Timezone Roulette | hot-path | `src/date.ts` | **fix now** — bundle with #2/#4 (same file, same refactor) |
| 9 | crime_00009 | Temporal Recklessness (7 calls in billing) | structural / testability | `src/billing.ts` | **defer** — falls out of #1 once `generateInvoice` is split + clock is injected from #2 |
| 10 | crime_00011 | Test That Proves Nothing | testability | `src/billing.test.ts` | **defer** — re-author after #1 lands; the test is meaningless against the current monolith anyway |

## Grouped view

- **structural**: #1 (`generateInvoice`), #2 (`date.ts` clock), #9 (`billing.ts` clock — defer, falls out of #1+#2)
- **frontend**: #3 (embedded raster in SVG)
- **IA**: #5 (missing AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md)
- **hot-path / portability** (not one of the five buckets — flagging): #4, #6, #7, #8
- **testability** (also outside the five buckets): #10 (defer)
- **git-history / duplication**: nothing in the top 10 — churn scores are all 0 (no git history on the fixture), and the duplication-adjacent findings (magic literals, nav source dup, route metadata drift) sit at #11+.

## Why this order

The first three are real `severity: high` items with high confidence — fix them first. After that, `date.ts` is the single highest-leverage file: #2, #4, #8 are one refactor (inject a clock + pick a date library + format with explicit zone), so they get bundled. The portability pair (#6, #7) is trivial and gates every non-author machine, so it's cheap to do now rather than defer. #5 is the only "fix now" that isn't about code correctness — it's there because every later agent-assisted fix in this list gets cheaper once `AGENTS.md` exists. #9 and #10 are deferred not because they don't matter, but because doing them before #1 wastes work.

Nothing in the top 10 warrants **ignore** — the lowest-ranked here is still `medium` and has a concrete cheap fix.
